# wsl-transdebian Copilot Instructions

## What this repo is

A static [reprepro](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/reprepro/reprepro.1.en.html)-managed Debian/Ubuntu apt package repository for WSL-specific packages, served via GitHub Pages at `https://arkane-systems.github.io/wsl-transdebian/apt/`.

There is no application code, build system, or test suite. All publishing is fully automated via GitHub Actions.

## Repository layout

- `apt/` — The live repository tree served by GitHub Pages. Do not edit files here manually; they are managed by the `apt-repo-update` action.
  - `apt/conf/distributions` — reprepro distribution definitions (codenames, architectures, signing key)
  - `apt/conf/incoming` — reprepro incoming config; `multiple_distributions` option allows one package to target several distros
  - `apt/pool/main/<letter>/<source>/` — Debian pool layout for `.deb`, `.dsc`, and `.tar.xz` files
  - `apt/dists/<codename>/` — Auto-generated `Packages`, `InRelease`, and `Release` files
  - `apt/wsl-transdebian.gpg` / `.asc` — Public GPG signing key (fingerprint `428AD1A0BA078ABB`)
- `updates/` — Drop new packages here (via PR) to have them ingested on the next push to `master`

## Publishing workflow

1. A contributor forks the repo, places `.deb` (and optionally `.dsc` + `.tar.xz`) in `updates/`, and opens a PR.
2. On merge to `master`, the `arkane-systems/apt-repo-update@v1.1` action runs, ingesting packages from `updates/` into `apt/` using reprepro, then commits and pushes the updated `apt/` tree.
3. GitHub Pages serves the updated `apt/` directory immediately.

Required repository secrets: `PAT` (GitHub personal access token), `TRANSDEBIAN_REPO_SIGNING_KEY`, `TRANSDEBIAN_REPO_PASSPHRASE`.

## Supported distributions and architectures

| Codename   | Distribution         | Status      |
|------------|----------------------|-------------|
| `buster`   | Debian 10            | Supported   |
| `bullseye` | Debian 11            | Supported   |
| `bookworm` | Debian 12            | Supported   |
| `sid`      | Debian (testing)     | Supported   |
| `trixie`   | Debian 13            | Supported   |
| `bionic`   | Ubuntu 18.04 LTS     | Supported   |
| `focal`    | Ubuntu 20.04 LTS     | Supported   |
| `jammy`    | Ubuntu 22.04 LTS     | Supported   |
| `stretch`  | Debian 9             | Deprecated  |

Supported architectures: `amd64`, `arm64` (defined in `apt/conf/distributions`).  
Note: the publish workflow currently only passes `amd64` to the `apt-repo-update` action.

## Key conventions

- **Pool naming**: packages follow the Debian pool layout — `pool/main/<first-letter-of-source>/<source-name>/<package>_<version>_<arch>.deb`.
- **Git LFS**: `*.tar.gz` files in `apt/` use Git LFS (see `apt/.gitattributes`). Ensure LFS is enabled before working with the repo locally.
- **Do not edit `apt/dists/` or `apt/db/` manually** — these are regenerated by reprepro on every publish run.
- **Adding a new distro**: update `apt/conf/distributions` (add a new stanza), `apt/conf/incoming` (`Allow:` list), and the `repo_supported_distro` list in `.github/workflows/main.yml`.
